title: Satureja marginata
taxonomy: Satureja marginata is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mountain savory. It is native to South America, specifically in the Andes mountains. The plant is classified under the genus Satureja, which includes various species of savory plants. Satureja marginata is often used as a medicinal herb, with properties that aid in digestion and provide anti-inflammatory effects. It is also used as a culinary herb, adding flavor to various dishes and drinks.
uses: Satureja marginata, also known as Chilean savory, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant is believed to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which could aid in the treatment of various health issues. Its leaves are often used to make teas and infusions, which are said to help alleviate symptoms of digestive problems, such as bloating and indigestion. Additionally, Satureja marginata is thought to have antiseptic properties, making it a potential remedy for wound healing and skin irritations. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of this plant.

constituents: Satureja marginata, also known as Mountain Savory, contains a variety of active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils. The essential oil of Mountain Savory has been found to contain compounds such as carvacrol, thymol, and γ-terpinene, which may contribute to its medicinal properties. Flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin have been identified as active constituents in Mountain Savory, and they may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Phenolic acids, including rosmarinic acid and chlorogenic acid, have also been detected in the plant. These active constituents may be responsible for the various medicinal and culinary uses of Satureja marginata.
